steps.title: Identify the independent clauses on each side of the blank
steps.content: The blank sits between two independent clauses: one ending with “...international photography festival in” and another beginning with “the exhibited photographs, with their vivid pastel colors, overexposed tones, and mirror like symmetry, captivated audiences.”

steps.title: Apply the rule for joining independent clauses
steps.content:
A semicolon separates two independent clauses; commas set off parenthetical phrases. Here, “for example” is parenthetical (clarifying that Italy is one example location), and the correct pattern is: (clause, parenthetical;) second clause.
Choice A places the comma before “for example” and the semicolon after it, exactly matching this pattern.
Choices B and D use only a comma between the clauses (comma splice), while choice C places the semicolon immediately after Italy, making “for example,” a transitional phrase introducing the second clause rather than a parenthetical clarifying the location.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
